summary: Static diff verification of V5.0.6308a (0adba2c38). All 4 residual fixes (R2 dynamic cooling, R3 bg-thread watchdog, R4/CR monotonic clock, R5 dust-qty ceiling) confirmed present in source. All 3 primary V5.0.6308 fixes and V5.0.6307/6306b baselines still intact. Read-only inspection — no compile attempted per operator instruction.

R2_dynamic_cooling.evidence: now = android.os.SystemClock.elapsedRealtime(); cycleMs>90_000L branch: coolMs=maxOf(120_000L, cycleMs*3L/2L); cycleMs>30_000L branch: coolMs=maxOf(60_000L, cycleMs*3L/2L). Both write supervisorCoolingUntilMs = maxOf(supervisorCoolingUntilMs, now+coolMs) — preserves any heavier active cooling.

R3_bghandler_watchdog.evidence: bgHandler.postDelayed({...}, 8_000L). Fallback buildEmergencyPipelineReport6308 executes inside bgHandler callback (off-Main); only deliverReport6308 → mainHandler.post for setPrimaryClip/Toast (required Main APIs).

R4_CR_monotonic_supervisorEffectiveCap.evidence: val nowMono = android.os.SystemClock.elapsedRealtime(); if (timeouts>=150) supervisorCoolingUntilMs = nowMono + 90_000L; val cooling = nowMono < supervisorCoolingUntilMs. All 4 supervisorCoolingUntilMs touches in the file use monotonic clock (lines 15921, 15922, 15991, 15997). Wall-clock `now` at 15912 is only used for the timeout-window bookkeeping, NOT for cooling reads/writes.

R5_dust_qty_ceiling.evidence: if (reconstructed.isFinite() && reconstructed > 0.0 && reconstructed < 1.0) { labelInc(ENTRY_PRICE_HEALED_FROM_COST_QTY_6308); reconstructed } — ceiling <1.0 SOL-per-token blocks astronomical basis from dust qty.

timebase_grep.evidence: grep supervisorCoolingUntilMs shows 4 non-comment sites (15921, 15922, 15991, 15997). All use SystemClock.elapsedRealtime(). No System.currentTimeMillis() read/write against supervisorCoolingUntilMs remains.

V5_0_6308_primary_fixes.evidence: SUPERVISOR_LEASE_TTL_MS = 12_000L @ line 15648. supervisorArmEmergencyThrottle called at cycleMs>15_000L @ line 15982 (telemetry-only, disarmed per 5.9.1332 comment — matches request). ENTRY_PRICE_HEALED_FROM_COST_QTY_6308 label emitted @ OpenPnlSanity.kt:115.

V5_0_6307_lane_unchoke_revert.evidence: LaneAutoPauseGuard.statusFor(laneName) called directly; block applies unconditionally when pauseState != null. No isPaperMode bypass in the FDG admission path.

V5_0_6306b_copy_thread.evidence: Thread({...}, "PipelineHealth-GenerateCopy-6308") calls PipelineHealthCollector.pasteSafeSnapshot(); no ReportingHub.buildTextAsync invocation in copyToClipboardAsync.

static_risk_assessment.operator_232s_scenario_recheck: Under the current source, a 232s cycle would: (1) log SUPERVISOR_EMERGENCY_THROTTLE_OBSERVED_DISARMED (observation-only, harmless); (2) enter cycleMs>90_000L branch → coolMs = maxOf(120_000L, 348_000L) = 348_000L (5m48s cooling on monotonic clock); (3) supervisorCoolingUntilMs advanced with maxOf() so a subsequent shorter cycle cannot shrink it; (4) supervisorEffectiveCap() reads the same monotonic clock → cap clamped to maxOf(8, base/3) for the full 348s. Cooling window now EXCEEDS the pathological cycle duration, closing the operator's original re-open-mid-stall gap. No residual code path found where wall-clock skew, Main-thread ANR, dust qty basis, or lane bypass could reproduce the 232s stall untreated.
residual_concerns: supervisorTimeoutWindowStartMs still uses System.currentTimeMillis() at line 15912/15950 — this is a SEPARATE 10-min timeout-count window (not cooling-related). NTP steps could reset the timeout counter, but the impact is bounded (worst case: one spurious 30/150 threshold cross) and does not affect the cooling latch which is now fully monotonic. Not urgent, but a future 'full monotonic timebase' pass could migrate this too.

The supervisorEffectiveCap timeout-count check `if (timeouts >= 150) supervisorCoolingUntilMs = nowMono + 90_000L` uses a fixed 90s — dynamic scaling was applied to cycle-based cooling only. If future ops report shows timeout-driven cooling too short, apply the same maxOf/dynamic pattern here. Not a blocker for the current fix.

critical_code_review_comments: BotService.kt is 25400 lines — extreme file-size violation. Recommend eventual split into SupervisorCoolingController, LeaseManager, CycleThrottle modules. Not blocking this release.
supervisorTimeoutWindowStartMs uses wall clock; consider migrating to monotonic in a follow-up pass for full timebase consistency (low priority, non-blocking).
context_for_next_testing_agent: V5.0.6308a static verification complete. All 4 residuals (R2 dynamic cooling, R3 bg-thread watchdog, R4+CR monotonic supervisorEffectiveCap, R5 dust-qty ceiling <1.0 SOL/token) verified present at BotService.kt:15910-16002, PipelineHealthActivity.kt:406-424, OpenPnlSanity.kt:108-116. supervisorCoolingUntilMs is now fully monotonic — 4 touch sites, 0 wall-clock. Only follow-up nit: supervisorTimeoutWindowStartMs (separate variable) still on wall clock, low impact.
rca of the issue: n/a — verification pass only; no bugs discovered.
